epigraph: A short lived fault or break from an expected flow of operation within a digital system.
quotes: The glitch is a wonderful experience of an interruption that shifts an object away from its ordinary form and discourse.
But once I named it, the momentum -the glitch- is no more...
Glitches do not exist outside of human perception. What was a glitch 10 years ago is not a glitch anymore.
source: Rosa Menkman, 'Glitch Studies Manifesto', Amsterdam/Cologne 2009/2010; repr. in Video Vortex Reader II, Institute of Network Cultures, 2011, pp. 336-347. Collected in 'The Glitch Moment(um)', Network Notebooks 04, INC, 2011, ISBN 978-90-816021-6-7, CC BY-NC-ND 3.0.
note: Verbatim sentences. The manifesto is typeset with irregular ('physiognomic') letter-spacing; whitespace here is normalized to the words themselves, wording unchanged. These ASCII bytes are the substrate that the work reads through the wrong flow.
